obligation_text: To the extent that it is strictly necessary for the purpose of ensuring bias detection and correction in relation to the high-risk AI systems, the providers of such systems may exceptionally process special categories of personal data, subject to appropriate safeguards for the fundamental rights and freedoms of natural persons.
scoring_text: Knowledge of lawful processing of special category personal data for AI bias detection, including legal basis assessment, necessity evaluation, safeguard implementation for fundamental rights, and documentation requirements under GDPR Article 9 and AI Act interaction.
_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Knowledge of lawful processing of special category personal data for AI bias detection, including legal basis assessment, necessity evaluation, safeguard implementation for fundamental rights, and documentation requirements under GDPR Article 9 and AI Act interaction.
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of AI regulatory compliance requirements, including EU AI Act conformity assessment obligations, prohibited use categories, and documentation requirements for high-risk AI systems.
comments: EU AI Act Art. 10(5) (obligation) intersects with WIDAI AG domain. AG-K-008 is the strongest match (STS: 0.470, cosine: 0.629). Secondary: AG-K-013 (AG, cosine: 0.607), RC-K-003 (RC, cosine: 0.594).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression. Primary method for all elements. Scores the competency_implication text (workforce competency interpretation of the regulatory requirement) against WIDAI KSA statements.
primary_method.significance: STS 4.7/10 (raw 0.470). Shared vocabulary: [act, ai, assessment, documentation, knowledge, requirements]. EU AI Act competency implication terms [9, article, basis, bias, category, data] vs WIDAI terms [categories, compliance, conformity, eu, high, obligations] define the semantic distance.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.894 (P=0.878, R=0.911). R exceeds P by 0.033 — the WIDAI KSA's 23 words are well-captured by the competency implication's 35 words, but the competency implication contains broader scope.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference classification. NLI entailment is systematically low for competency→competency pairs (mean ~0.007) because NLI measures strict logical implication, not semantic relatedness. Primary diagnostic value is in detecting contradiction.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.6%). Standard result for cross-framework competency mapping — the EU AI Act element and WIDAI KSA describe overlapping competencies without strict logical implication.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.629 > STS 0.470 (gap +0.159). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [act, ai, assessment, documentation, knowledge] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 23.8%. Shared tokens: [act, ai, assessment, documentation, knowledge, requirements]. EU AI Act-only: [9, article, basis, bias, category, data]. WIDAI-only: [categories, compliance, conformity, eu, high, obligations].

signals.detail: Bi-encoder/STS gap 0.159 with low STS 0.470. Candidate selection was likely driven by shared vocabulary rather than functional alignment. The functionally correct KSA may not have been in the top-5 candidates.
